title: Entity Extraction Memory
constraints: Entities must be extracted automatically from conversation, not manually tagged
The entity store must support at least people, places, and dates
Extracted entities must be available to the agent in subsequent turns
The extraction must not block or significantly slow the main response
hints: Use the LLM to extract entities by asking it to return structured JSON with entity types and values
Run entity extraction as a post-processing step after each assistant response
Store entities in a dictionary keyed by type (e.g., {'people': ['Alice', 'Bob'], 'places': ['Tokyo']}) and inject it into context

starterCode.langchain:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage, AIMessage, SystemMessage

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: No entity extraction — the agent doesn't track named entities
history = [SystemMessage(content="You are a helpful assistant.")]

def chat(user_input: str) -> str:
    history.append(HumanMessage(content=user_input))
    response = llm.invoke(history)
    history.append(response)
    return response.content

# Turn 1: Mentions people and places
print(chat("I met with Sarah and David in Tokyo last March to discuss the Q1 report."))

# Turn 2: References an entity from turn 1
print(chat("What city was my meeting in?"))

# Turn 3: More entities
print(chat("Next week I'm flying to Berlin to meet Professor Mueller on January 15th."))

# Turn 4: Query about people
print(chat("List all the people I've mentioned so far."))
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

# BUG: No entity extraction — the agent doesn't track named entities
agent = Agent(
    name="Assistant",
    instructions="You are a helpful assistant.",
)

history = []

def chat(user_input: str) -> str:
    history.append({"role": "user", "content": user_input})
    result = Runner.run_sync(agent, history)
    response_text = result.final_output
    history.append({"role": "assistant", "content": response_text})
    return response_text

# Turn 1: Mentions people and places
print(chat("I met with Sarah and David in Tokyo last March to discuss the Q1 report."))

# Turn 2: References an entity from turn 1
print(chat("What city was my meeting in?"))

# Turn 3: More entities
print(chat("Next week I'm flying to Berlin to meet Professor Mueller on January 15th."))

# Turn 4: Query about people
print(chat("List all the people I've mentioned so far."))
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew

# BUG: No entity extraction — the agent doesn't track named entities
assistant = Agent(
    role="Assistant",
    goal="Help users with their questions",
    backstory="You are a helpful assistant.",
)

history = []

def chat(user_input: str) -> str:
    history.append(f"User: {user_input}")
    task = Task(
        description="Conversation so far:\n" + "\n".join(history) + f"\n\nRespond to the latest message: {user_input}",
        expected_output="A helpful response",
        agent=assistant,
    )
    crew = Crew(agents=[assistant], tasks=[task])
    result = str(crew.kickoff())
    history.append(f"Assistant: {result}")
    return result

# Turn 1: Mentions people and places
print(chat("I met with Sarah and David in Tokyo last March to discuss the Q1 report."))

# Turn 2: References an entity from turn 1
print(chat("What city was my meeting in?"))

# Turn 3: More entities
print(chat("Next week I\'m flying to Berlin to meet Professor Mueller on January 15th."))

# Turn 4: Query about people
print(chat("List all the people I\'ve mentioned so far."))

evaluationCriteria.label: Extracts named entities
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent automatically identifies and stores people, places, and dates from conversation

evaluationCriteria.label: Entities are queryable
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent can list or reference previously extracted entities when asked

evaluationCriteria.label: Supports multiple entity types
evaluationCriteria.description: The extraction handles at least people, places, and dates correctly
